mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding aggregate labor income and consumer spending power, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positive in greater than 55% of months when PAYEMS increased, or the rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Once the federal funds rate exceeds 2.5%, restrictive monetary policy raises discount rates and debt-service costs enough to generate negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed dot plot signaling multiple hikes entering 2022
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in 50% or more of triggered observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5%, or the rank IC is insignificantly different from zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer prices and stagnation in industrial production produces stagflationary margin compression that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and INDPRO fails to add incremental rank IC of at least 0.02 beyond the main effects, or the bivariate signal rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions with unemployment below 5%, month-over-month retail sales growth signals resilient organic demand and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positive in greater than 55% of triggered instances when UNRATE is below 5% and RSAFS rises, or rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production index signals improving operating leverage and manufacturing earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are not positive following month-over-month INDPRO accelerations in at least 55% of observations, or rank IC is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1,400 thousand units indicates a contraction in residential investment and downstream demand, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 50% of observations when HOUST is below 1,400, or rank IC magnitude is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in unemployment and declines in retail sales capture a recessionary feedback loop between labor income and consumer demand, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The bivariate UNRATE-RSAFS signal does not outperform the stronger univariate signal by at least 0.03 in rank IC, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in a 5-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year consumer price inflation exceeding 6% forces rapid monetary tightening expectations and erodes real corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:CPI at multi-decade high of 7% entering 2022
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 55% of observations when CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 6%, or the rank IC magnitude is below 0.03.
